__reason: Live Woo sandbox credentials (WOO_BASE_URL / WOO_CONSUMER_KEY / WOO_CONSUMER_SECRET with WOO_WRITE_ENABLED=true) are NOT populated in the execution environment — only WOO_WRITE_ENABLED=false is set in .env. Per Plan 06-02 prompt instructions, the checkpoint is auto-approved and the expected request/response contract is synthesized from the Woo REST documentation + rudrastyh.com tutorial cited in 06-RESEARCH.md §4 + §Example 3.
__operator_validation_instructions: 1. Phase 7 cutover prep — operator MUST validate URL pass-through on the live Woo sandbox BEFORE enabling immediate-publish mode (config product_auto_create.mode=immediate) in production.
2. Populate .env: WOO_URL=<sandbox-url> / WOO_CONSUMER_KEY / WOO_CONSUMER_SECRET (ck_/cs_ prefix from Woo > Settings > Advanced > REST API).
3. Set WOO_WRITE_ENABLED=true — TEMPORARILY; revert immediately after.
4. Host a known-public test image at a URL Woo can reach — e.g. tests/Fixtures/ProductAutoCreate/sample.jpg uploaded to S3 or copied to public/images/.
6. Expected: response carries `images[0].id` (Woo-assigned int) + `images[0].src` pointing to `.../wp-content/uploads/YYYY/MM/sample.jpg` (NOT the original URL).

7. If validated, record the actual Woo-returned id + uploads URL into this file (replace the synthesized values below) + flip __synthesized=false. If it DIVERGES (e.g. Woo auto-rejects the URL), raise a blocker before enabling immediate-publish mode.
8. Set WOO_WRITE_ENABLED=false again. Delete the test product via `app(WooClient::class)->delete('/products/<id>', ['force'=>true])`.
__inference_sources: 06-RESEARCH.md §Summary — Woo REST URL pass-through finding (images[*].src accepts a public URL, Woo downloads it)
Woo REST API docs (woocommerce-rest-api-docs/trunk/source/includes/v3/_products.md) — images[] array shape + response semantics

headers_required: Authorization: Basic <base64(CK:CS)>  (consumer key + consumer secret — existing Phase 2 WooClient auth)

passthrough_signals: Response images[0].id is a FRESH Woo-assigned media-library integer (not present in request).
Response images[0].src points to /wp-content/uploads/YYYY/MM/ — Woo's OWN hosting, NOT the URL we sent. This is the unambiguous URL-pass-through success marker.
The outbound URL we sent (request.body.images[0].src) MUST stay alive for several seconds post-POST because Woo's download is asynchronous (cron or background worker).
NO binary upload, NO multipart, NO base64 data URL, NO /wp-json/wp/v2/media hop — the entire contract uses only the existing WooClient::post('/products', $payload) surface.
plan_03_consumer_notes: Plan 03's CreateWooProductJob merges ImagePayloadBuilder::build($product, $publicUrl) output into the top-level Woo product payload: $payload = array_merge($basePayload, $imagePayload); where $imagePayload is ['images' => [...]] or ['images' => []].

After the POST, Plan 03 may re-fetch GET /products/{id} if it needs to confirm Woo's async image download completed — but for v1 draft-first (AUTO-07), the POST response's images[0].id being non-null is sufficient evidence.

If Plan 03 later wants to PUT an image onto an existing Woo product (e.g. ProcessAutoCreateImageJob running AFTER CreateWooProductJob), the same images[] shape works: PUT /products/{id} with body {images: [{src: 'new-public-url', name: '...', alt: '...'}]}.
